scenario_results.reason: Unsigned authorization and unverified requester identity blocked disclosure, preserved minimum-necessary posture, and routed to HIM/ROI or privacy owner.
Incomplete EHR access evidence produced privacy investigation intake with missing audit/IAM/role facts and no final breach determination.
Broad PHI export for convenience was refused under minimum-necessary controls with owner escalation and safe alternative requirements.
Complete authorization, identity, scope, recipient, and ROI evidence produced a traceable privacy-review intake summary.
Formal legal signoff and unsupported disclosure approval were refused and routed to legal/privacy officer authority.
Missing jurisdiction, tenant policy, source hierarchy, role, identity-proofing rule, and audit hooks triggered low-confidence orchestrator return.
Conflicting state, Part 2, and tenant policy constraints were recorded as unresolved and escalated without confident synthesis.
Clinical amendment and medication harm judgment was routed to licensed clinical/HIM authority with no medical advice.
Payer eligibility, authorization, referral, and service-date gaps blocked privacy handling and routed to patient access or payer operations.
Conflicting accounting, ROI, and audit records produced an evidence checklist and HIM/privacy escalation rather than a final accounting response.
Tenant terminology was normalized to public baseline with source-system mapping, assumptions, and required fact validation.
Overdue training and missing attestation produced remediation escalation without deciding workforce sanctions or breach status.

notes: Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all compliance-controls-and-governance abilities, and all healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities.

The evaluation suite covers authorization and identity gaps, breach-sensitive incomplete evidence, minimum-necessary conflict, legal signoff refusal, tenant policy conflict, clinical boundary escalation, payer dependency handling, accounting evidence conflict, tenant adaptation, and remediation escalation.
Package remains explicit that runtime strategy is specification-based and does not claim retained implementation authority.
